# setUpWithError()

**Instance Method**

Provides an opportunity to reset state and to throw errors before calling each test method in a test case.

## Declaration

```swift
func setUpWithError() throws
```

## Discussion

Before each test begins, `XCTest` calls [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTest/setUp(completion:)] for asynchronous state preparation, then this method, followed by [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTest/setUp()].

If state preparation might throw errors, override [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTest/setUp(completion:)] or this method. `XCTest` marks the test failed when it catches errors, or skipped when it catches `XCTSkip`.

## Setting Up and Tearing Down

- **setUp(completion:)**: Provides an opportunity to reset state asynchronously and handle errors before calling each test method in a test case.
- **setUp()**: Provides an opportunity to reset state before calling each test method in a test case.
- **tearDown(completion:)**: Provides an opportunity to perform cleanup asynchronously and handle errors after each test method in a test case ends.
- **tearDownWithError()**: Provides an opportunity to perform cleanup and to throw errors after each test method in a test case ends.
- **tearDown()**: Provides an opportunity to perform cleanup after each test method in a test case ends.
